info.title: dotnet-deb
info.description:
Create Debian and Ubuntu installers (.deb files ) of your .NET Core projects straight from the command line.

Once you've installed this package as a .NET CLI tool, run dotnet deb to generate a .deb file which contains the publish output of your .NET Project.

See https://github.com/qmfrederik/dotnet-packaging/ for more information on how to use dotnet deb.

options.name: runtime
options.description: Target runtime of the Debian/Ubuntu installer package. The target runtime has to be specified in the project file.

options.name: framework
options.description: Target framework of the Debian/Ubuntu installer package. The target framework has to be specified in the project file.

options.name: configuration
options.description: Target configuration of the Debian/Ubuntu installer package. The default for most projects is 'Debug'.

options.name: output-dir
options.description: The output directory to place built packages in. The default is the output directory of your project.

options.name: version-suffix
options.description: Defines the value for the $(VersionSuffix) property in the project.

options.name: no-restore
options.description: Do not restore the project before building.

arguments.name: project
arguments.description: The project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.
